title: Cassinia petrapendula
taxonomy: Cassinia petrapendula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is native to Australia. It is a member of the genus Cassinia, which is a group of Australian shrubs. Cassinia petrapendula is further classified as a shrub that grows to a height of around 1-2 meters. It is known for its small white or yellow flowers that are arranged in a compact cluster.
uses: Cassinia petrapendula has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for various purposes.
